behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam between evaluate-stage logic and artifact persistence (artifact key selection + write target). MOCK-MISS: the failure is in handoff to storage, not pure local parsing. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required OS/network/subprocess, so not System. LLM-DEP: correctness is key routing, not model quality, so not Agentic. MULTI-STEP: single stage behavior, not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only requires evaluate-stage artifact slot correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with a real in-process artifact repository (or test DB) and assert: `grounding` is written, `plan` is untouched by evaluate. Verify persisted artifact keys/values, not just function return.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam across implement orchestration + artifact read/write paths. MOCK-MISS: bug appears when read path and write path are wired together (consume `grounding`, emit `plan`). REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP: this behavior is about artifact plumbing and destination slot, not judging model output quality. MULTI-STEP: single stage flow with optional input, so below Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text targets IO contract ('reads grounding', 'writes ... to plan'), not semantic quality.
behaviors.test_description: Preload `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow end-to-end with real local artifact storage and stage wiring, assert implement reads grounding context and writes execution output to `plan` while preserving `grounding`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam between diagnosis-presence check and artifact-write side effects. MOCK-MISS: real failure is conditional suppression of write through integration of issue-content reader + artifact writer. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: pass/fail is whether `plan` was written, not model judgment. MULTI-STEP: single deep-dive stage condition. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow since behavior text is side-effect suppression in one stage.
behaviors.test_description: Seed issue content with existing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with real in-process storage, assert no new `plan` artifact write occurs (or existing `plan` unchanged) and no redundant overwrite side effect is emitted.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: multi-step orchestration seam across chained stages and cross-step artifact propagation. MULTI-STEP: explicitly a full chain run; minimum boundary is workflow-level pipeline behavior. MOCK-MISS: isolated stage tests can pass while cross-step propagation/regression fails. LLM-DEP: likely agent-produced intermediate content contributes, but even without quality scoring, the key failure is end-to-end pipeline carryover across steps. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires full multi-step run and reflection of earlier grounding in final output.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real chained pipeline `deep-dive -> evaluate/write-plan -> implement` in one run context, then assert final `plan` contains/uses grounding signals created earlier (traceable marker or structured field), proving cross-step carry-through.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam for fallback branch + artifact isolation guarantees. MOCK-MISS: corruption bugs emerge when implement flow interacts with artifact store state, not just local branch logic. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: correctness criterion is safe fallback and artifact integrity, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: single-stage fallback behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text focuses on storage safety and fallback mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Start with artifact store containing unrelated artifacts and no `grounding`; run implement stage; assert a valid `plan` is created, no exceptions, and pre-existing non-plan artifacts remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
